request.question: Break down Jahez's FY2025 unit economics: GMV, take rate, AOV, and why did net income compress 61%?

events.text: **Jahez's FY2025 unit economics: strong demand, thinning profit.**
- **GMV** reached SAR 7,245.2M, up 10.8% YoY[[1]], volume is still compounding.

events.quote: GMV 7,245.2 (+10.8% YoY), Group Financial Summary FY2025

events.text: - **Take rate** rose to roughly 15.4%, computed as commission revenue ÷ GMV[[2]]; monetization is the bright spot.
- **AOV** ticked up to SAR 64.9 per order[[3]], a modest +5.2%.
So the top of the funnel is healthy. The pressure is all below it:
**Net income attributable to shareholders fell 61.2% to SAR 73.0M**[[4]].

events.quote: Net Profit attributable to shareholders of the parent 73.0 (-61.2% YoY)

events.text: The bulk is one-time, roughly **SAR 55.4M of Q4 one-offs** (Red Color and Marn impairments, an Others-segment ECL charge, and Snoonu deal expenses)[[5]], but adjusted profitability still softened as delivery costs and D&A outran the take-rate gains.

events.quote: Q4 one-offs: Red Color 12.2m, Marn goodwill 11.8m, Others-segment ECL 23.4m, Snoonu deal expenses ~8m (≈55.4m)

events.text: Bottom line: a compounding franchise absorbing a real but mostly non-recurring margin shock, the number to underwrite is the FY2026 exit take rate, not the FY2025 net income.
